mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show a positive mean during rate hike periods with a t-statistic greater than 2.0, or the rank correlation between FEDFUNDS changes and forward returns is positive over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A labor market slack threshold breach above 4.5% unemployment signals deteriorating consumer spending capacity and rising recession probability, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal triggers but S&P 500 forward 5-day returns remain positive in more than 55% of instances, or the unemployment rate crosses the threshold without any subsequent equity drawdown exceeding 2%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand growth, which drives corporate revenue acceleration and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day equity returns, with an out-of-sample hit rate below 45% across all post-2010 expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production signals immediate supply-side bottlenecks and margin compression, causing rapid negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening cycles are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in over 60% of cases, or the predictive slope coefficient turns positive in a walk-forward regression.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation above a 0.4% monthly threshold forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, increasing equity risk premiums and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL exceeds the 0.4% monthly threshold but S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of observations, or the inflation threshold shows zero predictive power in a logistic regression model.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising corporate profit expectations, which consistently drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth rates show a negative or statistically insignificant correlation with 20-day forward S&P 500 returns over any 24-month rolling window, or the signal yields a negative Sharpe ratio in live trading.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between slowing real GDP growth and declining retail sales indicates weakening aggregate demand that precedes earnings downgrades, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The demand divergence signal triggers but is followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in over 55% of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance with a p-value greater than 0.10 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase signals capacity utilization improvements and margin expansion, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during recovery regimes fail to predict positive 20-day equity returns, with a mean forward return below zero and a t-statistic less than -1.5 over the sample period.
